title: Nadutariappar Temple
tagline: Nadutariappar Temple — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Nadutariappar Temple, Kanrappur is a Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva located at Kanrappur near Tiruvarur, Tamil Nadu, India.
overview.description: Nadutariappar Temple, Kanrappur is a Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva located at Kanrappur near Tiruvarur, Tamil Nadu, India. The temple is incarnated by the hymns of Appar and is classified as Paadal Petra Sthalam. Shiva is worshiped as Nadutariappar, and is represented by the lingam and his consort Parvati is depicted as Srivalli Nayagi. The presiding deity is revered in the 7th century Tamil Saiva canonical work, the Tevaram, written by Tamil poet saints known as the nayanars and classified as Paadal Petra Sthalam.

topPlaces.name: Kizhavenmani
topPlaces.description: Kizhavenmani (also spelled Kilvenmani and Keezhvenmani) is a village in Nagapattinam district of Tamil Nadu state in India. The village is about 25 km from the district headquarters town of Nagapattinam.

topPlaces.name: Deva Pureeswarar Temple
topPlaces.description: Deva Pureeswarar Temple (தேவபுரீஸ்வரர் கோயில் ) is a Hindu temple in Thevur in the Tiruvarur District of Tamil Nadu, India. The presiding deity is Shiva.

topPlaces.name: Valivalam
topPlaces.description: Valivalam is a small town in Nagapattinam district, Tamil Nadu, India. Now the Valivalam village have the new small man helping nature-made forest which is initiated by pasumai valivalam amaippu (environmental volunteers group) in July 2019 with 2500+ trees of 70-75 variety of species in nearly 20000 sq.ft of formerly degraded land by using akira miyawakki method, especially majority of fruit bearing species to attract birds.

topPlaces.name: Kannayariamudayar Temple, Thirukkarayil
topPlaces.description: Kannayiranathar Swamy Temple, Thirukkarayil (also called Tirukarahil or Tirukarayil) is a Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva located in Thirukaravasal in Nagapattinam district of Tamil Nadu, India. Constructed in the Dravidian style of architecture, the temple is believed to have been built during the Cholas period in the 9th century CE. Shiva is worshipped as Kannayiranathar and his consort Parvathi as Kailasanayaki.

topPlaces.name: Adiyakkamangalam
topPlaces.description: Adiyakkamangalam is a village panchayat located in the south Indian state of Tamil Nadu. Located at 5 km from Tiruvarur (East), 15 km from Nagappattinam, it is on the east border of Tiruvarur district.

topPlaces.name: Sundaresvarar Temple, Kundayur
topPlaces.description: Sundaresvarar Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to the deity Shiva, located at Kundaiyur in Nagapattinam district in Tamil Nadu (India).

topPlaces.name: Kilvelur taluk
topPlaces.description: Kilvelur taluk is a taluk of Nagapattinam district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The headquarters of the taluk is the town of Kilvelur.

topPlaces.name: Kilvelur
topPlaces.description: Kilvelur is a panchayat town in kilvelur taluk in the district of Nagapattinam district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Nadutariappar Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Nadutariappar Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Nadutariappar Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Tiruchirappalli International Airport (108 km) and the nearest railway station is Tiruchirappalli Junction (111 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Nadutariappar Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Nadutariappar Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Nadutariappar Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Nadutariappar Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Nadutariappar Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kizhavenmani, Deva Pureeswarar Temple, Valivalam, Kannayariamudayar Temple, Thirukkarayil, Adiyakkamangalam.
